title: bromus scoparius var. rubens
intro: Bromus scoparius var. rubens, also known as broom sedge, is a perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to Europe, North America, and Asia. The medicinal properties of broom sedge lie in its ability to act as a diuretic, helping to reduce fluid retention and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract issues. The health benefits of consuming broom sedge include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of broom sedge can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
